Retirement Planning for NRIs – Key Strategies and Insights
Non-resident Indians (NRIs) often move internationally for career growth, better earning potential and other reasons. However, when it comes to retirement, they might want to return to India and stay with their near and distant families. Alternatively, they can choose to settle abroad with no plans to return to India even after retirement.Whatever the choice, planning for retirement is important so that you can retire without financial worries. Whether you are settling abroad or in India, you need an adequate retirement corpus to fund your living expenses and medical bills. As such, when you are working, you can start building your retirement fund, one step at a time, to create a good corpus by the time you actually retire.When planning for retirement, though, some things should be borne in mind for drafting an effective plan. Let’s understand what these things are.
What are NRI Retirement Plans?
NRI retirement plans are tools that can help NRIs save up for their golden years. These plans allow NRIs to save a part of their income during their active working life and create a corpus to meet their expenses post-retirement.
NRI retirement plans are goal-specific plans which aim to create a retirement corpus. Under some plans, there are stable annuity payouts too, so that NRIs can create a regular source of income after they stop working.
There are different types of NRI retirement plans in the market. Life insurance also offers NRI retirement plans called annuity plans. There are two types of annuity plans –
Deferred annuity plans – These plans help NRIs to save up for their retirement.
Immediate annuity plans – These plans are meant for retiring NRIs who can pay a lump sum and start receiving steady annuity payouts immediately to create a source of income.
NRIs can choose a suitable retirement plan and plan ahead to meet their post-retirement expenses.
Why NRIs Need Retirement Planning?
NRIs face unique challenges when planning for retirement. If they are planning to retire back in India, they need to build a corpus to support their life after returning. On the other hand, if they plan to retire in the country they live in, they need to build up a corpus to meet the expenses of that country.
Moreover, NRIs have access to retirement plans in India and abroad. While retirement plans in India can help them diversify their portfolio across boundaries, retirement plans abroad can help them build a corpus in their home country. However, there are expenses and tax implications to consider.
Retirement planning for NRIs is important as it can help them explore low-cost options in India or abroad. Moreover, they have to factor in currency fluctuations to create a good corpus without a high exchange rate risk.
It is recommended for NRIs to start retirement planning in advance so that they can factor in the challenges and build a suitable corpus for their golden years.
Things to Remember When Planning for Retirement
1. Time to retire and life expectancy:
Time to retire is basically the age at which you want to retire. For instance, if you are 35 currently and you are planning to retire at 65, the time to retire would be 30 years.
Estimating the time to retire would help you assess your investment horizon so that you can choose suitable investment avenues. Life expectancy is the maximum age at which you expect to live. This would help you understand the number of years your retirement corpus should last after you retire. For instance, if you retire at 65 and you expect to survive till 75, your life expectancy is 10 years, and you need a retirement fund optimal enough to meet your expenses for the said duration.
Estimating life expectancy correctly is a challenge, as life is unpredictable. So, it's better to assume that you will live till 99 or 100 years of age and plan accordingly so that the retirement corpus doesn’t fall short.
2. Place to retire:
The place to retire is the country or city where you would live after you retire. This is an important consideration since the living expenses, inflation and medical costs vary across geographies. For instance, retiring in the USA would be more expensive compared to retiring in India. Even in India, retiring in a metropolis like Mumbai or Delhi might be more expensive than retiring in a Tier II or Tier III city.
Knowing the place to retire would help you build up a suitable relocation nest with which you can fund your retirement home.
3. Inflation rate:
Know the inflation rate of the country you wish to retire in. Keep it under consideration when planning the retirement fund. Find out what the average living expenses 20 or 30 years later when you retire, after factoring in inflation. When you know the expected expenses, after inflation, you can build up an optimal retirement corpus which would be able to meet the inflated expenses.
4. Retirement goals:
Assess your goals after you retire. They can be taking a world trip, buying a luxury apartment, or ticking off items on their bucket list. Based on these goals, you can estimate the retirement corpus you would need.
5. Risk profile
Know your risk profile, i.e., whether you are tolerant of investment risks or you want to avoid them. This would help in choosing the right investment avenues which align with your risk preference. Moreover, as you grow older, it may be preferred to switch from risky investment avenues, like equity, and move towards safer avenues, like debt, to protect the corpus accumulated and the returns earned thereon.
6. Exchange rate
If you are planning to return to India or move to another country after retirement, keep the exchange rate in mind, as your corpus would be converted into another currency.
How to Plan for Retirement?
Keep the aforementioned points in mind when considering a retirement plan. Some retirement planning tips may include –
1. Start early –
Start saving early so that you can save affordably, and the long-term horizon would add to the corpus through compounding of returns.
2. Save systematically –
Save in a disciplined manner, regularly, to create an optimal retirement corpus. Do not invest haphazardly. Make savings and investments a habit and follow it regularly.
3. Invest in suitable avenues –
Choose suitable investment avenues to plan your retirement corpus. A deferred annuity helps one lock the future annuity rates at the current date and thereby financially secure their retirement goals. Moreover, annuity plans allow annuity payments, which can create a source of regular and lifelong income after retirement.
4. Diversify your portfolio –
Portfolio diversification can help you spread out the investment risks and enhance the return potential of your investments. So, invest in equity and debt instruments and create a good mix of investments.
Types of Retirement Plans
As mentioned earlier, there are different types of retirement plans available in the market. Some of the most popular NRI retirement plans are as follows –
Deferred annuity plans
Deferred annuity plans are savings plans which aim to help you build up a retirement corpus when you are working. You can choose a policy tenure depending on the time to retirement and save up over the term to create a retirement corpus.
Deferred annuity plans are offered as traditional endowment plans and ULIPs. Depending on your investment strategy and risk profile, you can choose a suitable plan and save up for your golden years.
Immediate annuity plans
Under immediate annuity plans, you start receiving a guaranteed and stable annuity immediately after buying the policy. You have to pay a single premium, called the purchase price, to buy the plan. Thereafter, the annuity payouts start and continue till life.
Immediate annuity plans aim to create a steady source of income and offer different annuity payouts to choose from. With these retirement plans, NRIs can enjoy a stable financial income even in their older ages.
National Pension System (NPS)
The NPS scheme is a government-backed retirement plan for NRIs and residents, which helps you save up for retirement and then receive regular annuity payouts. The plan offers market-linked returns and tax benefits, too.
Pension Plans:
Pension plans often combine two benefits, i.e. investment along with insurance protection. When you contribute regularly, your money builds up step by step into a retirement corpus. This corpus then gives you a steady income after you stop working.
Key Features of Retirement Plans
Some key features differentiate NRI retirement plans from other investment avenues. Some of these features are as follows –
Stable Income
Plans like annuity plans are designed to offer annuity payouts. These annuity payouts create a stable source of income during retirement when you might not have an active salary or business income, making retirement planning for NRIs easy and effective.
Flexible Payout Options Tailored for NRIs
On maturity, NRI retirement plans help you commute(withdraw) up to a specified part of the accumulated corpus in cash. You can use this commuted value to meet any financial need that you might have. The remaining corpus can be used to receive annuity payouts either immediately or after a deferment period, subject to specific conditions.
This flexibility helps you plan your retirement with ease.
Tax-Deferred Investment Growth
Retirement planning for NRIs includes tax-saving options too. For instance, life insurance deferred annuity plans allow you to claim a tax deduction on the premium paid under Section 80CCC. Similarly, the NPS scheme also allows tax-deductible investments under Section 80C to help you build a tax-efficient retirement corpus.
Things NRIs Should Know Before Investing in a Retirement Plan
While there are different types of NRI retirement plans, here are some factors and things to know before you invest –
Retirement horizon
The first thing to assess is the time to retirement, i.e., after how many years you plan to retire. This would help you estimate your investment horizon and choose an avenue accordingly. For instance, if you are retiring after 30 years, you can plan for the long term, but if retirement is just 5 years away, you need a short-term avenue.
Place of retirement
The place where you intend to retire is also important to consider. This is because lifestyle expenses vary across countries. India can be a cost-effective option for retirement compared to the US or UK. As such, the retirement corpus needed in India can be lower than the retirement corpus needed when you plan to retire abroad.
Inflation rate
Inflation increases the prices of goods and services and reduces the purchasing power of money. Over time, inflation is expected to increase your lifestyle expenses after retirement. So, when doing retirement planning for NRIs, you should factor in inflation and create a corpus that would be able to tackle inflation and provide you with the funds you need.
Retirement goals
If you have specific goals after retirement, you need to factor them in as they increase the corpus needed. For instance, if you want to buy a home in India or take a world tour after retirement, you need to add their expected costs into your retirement corpus.
Risk profile
Choose an investment avenue based on your risk appetite. For instance, if you love taking risks for the prospect of returns, you can choose ULIP annuity plans and allocate the premium to equity funds. For NRIs with a lower risk appetite, debt funds would be a better choice. However, if you are completely risk-averse and want a secure corpus, you can choose endowment-oriented annuity plans.
Tax implications
The retirement plans that you choose might have tax implications in India and abroad. Understand the tax implications so that you can create a tax-efficient retirement corpus.
Exchange rate fluctuations
If you are investing in India from abroad, you need to factor in the exchange rate fluctuations that would affect your investments.
Key takeaways
NRIs can consider retirement plans to plan for a financially comfortable life in their golden years.
When it comes to retirement plans, NRIs can invest in India or abroad depending on their needs.
NRIs can choose from life insurance annuity plans or the NPS scheme to fund their retirement.
Retirement plans help NRIs create a stable source of income in their golden years and can also offer tax benefits.
Consider the time to retirement, retirement place, inflation rate, expected goals, risk appetite, and other factors when planning a retirement corpus.
Conclusion Retirement planning is no rocket science. All you need is a little understanding of your preferences and goals. Start your retirement planning today to build up a suitable nest egg for a financially independent retired life. For securing lifelong incomes, a life insurance pension plan might be a suitable choice. You can choose a deferred annuity plan and save up for retirement. Moreover, if you choose a ULIP, you can diversify your investment across different funds and earn market-linked returns.So, use the points discussed above and start your retirement planning journey without delay.
FAQs
What makes investing in retirement plans in India a good option for NRIs?Retirement plans in India can give NRIs various benefits, like –
Exposure to Indian markets
Tax benefits on the investment made
Lower life insurance premiums compared to international plans
Different options to choose from.Given these benefits, NRIs can invest in retirement plans in India and plan for their golden years.
Who can buy retirement plans designed for NRIs?NRIs living in other countries are eligible to buy NRI retirement plans from India.
Which documents do I need to submit for an NRI retirement plan application?The documents depend on the type of NRI retirement plan that you choose. That being said, some of the common documents include the following –
Identity proof
Age proof
Proof of overseas address
Copy of passport with Visa
Income proof, etc.
How can I identify the most suitable NRI retirement plan for my goals?The best NRI retirement plan would depend on –
Your financial goals
Risk-taking ability
Tax implications
Expected returns
Inflation, etc.You can factor in these parameters and find a plan that allows you to create a tax-efficient corpus.
Do NRIs get any tax advantages when investing in retirement plans in India?Yes, NRIs can enjoy tax benefits on their investments depending on which investment avenue they choose. For instance, life insurance annuity plans allow tax deduction under Section 80CCC on the premiums that NRIs pay1. The death benefit is tax-free. Plus, on maturity, if NRIs commute a part of their corpus, the commuted amount, subject to a specified limit, would be allowed as a tax-free income under Section 10(10A)2.
